content.text:
I now have a thorough understanding of every relevant piece of the codebase. Let me synthesize the implementation plan.

---

## E2: Neo-Brutalist Design System -- Implementation Plan

### Context Summary

The current codebase (E1) has:
- 6 UI components (`DawShell`, `Toolbar`, `panels` x3, `ClickToStart`, `CrossOriginError`) all using inline React `style={}` objects
- No CSS files whatsoever in `src/`
- JetBrains Mono referenced as a string in inline styles but never loaded via `@font-face` or link
- Hardcoded hex colors throughout (`#000`, `#111`, `#333`, `#666`, `#888`, `#fff`, `#ff3333`)
- 8px-ish spacing used inconsistently (some 16px, some 32px, some 48px)
- No keyboard shortcut system
- No focus styles
- 52 tests passing, all using `@testing-library/react` with `data-testid` and semantic queries
- Zustand store at `src/state/store.ts` with transport + engine status
- Path aliases: `@ui/*` resolves to `src/ui/*`

The spec calls for a **custom** design system (explicitly "NOT shadcn/ui"), using **CSS custom properties** for tokens (not CSS-in-JS). The advisory brief flagged this as high-effort (P1-14) but it was deliberately overridden in the epic requirements.

---

### File Structure

All new files under `src/ui/` with this layout:

```
src/ui/
  tokens/
    tokens.css              -- CSS custom properties (the single source of truth)
    tokens.ts               -- TypeScript constants mirroring CSS tokens for canvas/JS use
    tokens.test.ts          -- Validate TS constants match CSS values; test token completeness
  primitives/
    Button.tsx              -- <button> with neo-brutalist states
    Button.test.tsx
    Select.tsx              -- Custom dropdown (styled native <select> or custom listbox)
    Select.test.tsx
    Modal.tsx               -- Dialog with backdrop, trap focus
    Modal.test.tsx
    Tooltip.tsx             -- Positioned tooltip on hover/focus
    Tooltip.test.tsx
    ContextMenu.tsx         -- Right-click context menu
    ContextMenu.test.tsx
    Toggle.tsx              -- On/off toggle switch
    Toggle.test.tsx
  controls/
    RotaryKnob.tsx          -- Canvas-based rotary control with drag
    RotaryKnob.test.tsx
    Fader.tsx               -- Vertical fader/slider
    Fader.test.tsx
    VuMeter.tsx             -- Canvas-based VU/peak meter
    VuMeter.test.tsx
    DrumPad.tsx             -- Trigger pad (button with velocity)
    DrumPad.test.tsx
    StepButton.tsx          -- Sequencer step toggle
    StepButton.test.tsx
  keyboard/
    command-registry.ts     -- CommandRegistry class
    command-registry.test.ts
    shortcut-map.ts         -- Default shortcut bindings
    shortcut-map.test.ts
    useKeyboardShortcuts.ts -- React hook for global listener
    useKeyboardShortcuts.test.ts
  hooks/
    useReducedMotion.ts     -- prefers-reduced-motion media query hook
    useReducedMotion.test.ts
  global.css                -- Reset, font loading, body defaults, reduced-motion
  index.ts                  -- Re-export barrel (updated)
```

---

### Phase 1: Tokens + Global CSS Foundation

**Goal**: Establish the design token layer that everything else builds on. Zero runtime, pure CSS custom properties.

**Task 1.1: `src/ui/tokens/tokens.css`**

Define all CSS custom properties on `:root`:

- **Colors**: `--color-black: #000`, `--color-white: #FFF`, `--color-blue: #0066FF` (electric blue), `--color-pink: #FF2D6F` (hot pink), `--color-green: #39FF14` (acid green), `--color-amber: #FFB800`, `--color-red: #FF0000` (pure red). Plus grays: `--color-gray-900: #111`, `--color-gray-700: #333`, `--color-gray-500: #666`, `--color-gray-300: #999`, `--color-gray-100: #E5E5E5`.
- **Typography**: `--font-mono: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace`, `--font-sans: system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif`. Size scale on 8px grid: `--text-xs: 10px`, `--text-sm: 12px`, `--text-base: 14px`, `--text-lg: 16px`, `--text-xl: 20px`, `--text-2xl: 24px`, `--text-3xl: 32px`, `--text-4xl: 48px`.
- **Spacing**: `--space-1: 4px`, `--space-2: 8px`, `--space-3: 12px`, `--space-4: 16px`, `--space-6: 24px`, `--space-8: 32px`, `--space-12: 48px`, `--space-16: 64px` (8px grid, with 4px half-step).
- **Borders**: `--border-width: 2px`, `--border-color: var(--color-gray-700)`, `--border: var(--border-width) solid var(--border-color)`.
- **Shadows**: None (Neo-Brutalist rejects drop shadows).
- **Focus ring**: `--focus-ring: 2px solid var(--color-blue)`, `--focus-offset: 2px`.
- **Transitions**: `--transition-fast: 100ms`, `--transition-normal: 200ms` (used only when reduced-motion is not active).

Note on exact hex values: The spec says "electric blue, hot pink, acid green, amber, pure red" but does not prescribe exact hex values except for `#000` and `#FFF`. The values above are chosen for WCAG AA contrast against black backgrounds (the primary ground). These should be validated during implementation -- particularly `--color-green` which at `#39FF14` has excellent contrast on black (>7:1) but poor contrast on white. Since this is a dark-ground-dominant DAW, that's acceptable.

**Task 1.2: `src/ui/tokens/tokens.ts`**

TypeScript constants object that mirrors the CSS tokens. This is needed because canvas-based components (`RotaryKnob`, `VuMeter`) cannot read CSS custom properties during `CanvasRenderingContext2D` draw calls -- they need raw JS values. Export as a `const` assertion (`as const`) for maximum type safety.

```ts
export const tokens = {
  color: {
    black: "#000000",
    white: "#FFFFFF",
    blue: "#0066FF",
    // ...
  },
  space: {
    1: 4,
    2: 8,
    // ...  (numeric values in px for canvas math)
  },
  font: {
    mono: "'JetBrains Mono', monospace",
    sans: "system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif",
  },
} as const;

export type ThemeTokens = typeof tokens;
```

**Task 1.3: `src/ui/tokens/tokens.test.ts`**

Test that the TypeScript tokens object has all expected categories and values. Validate that every color is a valid hex string, every spacing value is a multiple of 4, font strings are non-empty. This is a "contract test" ensuring the TS mirror does not drift from the CSS.

**Task 1.4: `src/ui/global.css`**

- CSS reset (minimal: `*, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }`)
- `@font-face` for JetBrains Mono (or Google Fonts `@import` -- check if the font is already available; the existing inline styles reference it by name but never load it)
- `body` defaults: `background: var(--color-black); color: var(--color-white); font-family: var(--font-mono);`
- `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block that sets `--transition-fast: 0ms; --transition-normal: 0ms;` and `* { animation-duration: 0ms !important; transition-duration: 0ms !important; }`
- Focus visible styles: `:focus-visible { outline: var(--focus-ring); outline-offset: var(--focus-offset); }`

**Task 1.5: Wire CSS into the app**

- Import `src/ui/tokens/tokens.css` and `src/ui/global.css` in `src/main.tsx` (the entry point). Vite handles CSS imports.
- Add JetBrains Mono font link to `index.html` `<head>` (Google Fonts CDN is the simplest path)
- Update `index.html` `<title>` to "BRUTALWAV"

**Task 1.6: Migrate existing components from inline styles to CSS tokens**

Replace hardcoded values in `DawShell`, `Toolbar`, `panels`, `ClickToStart`, `CrossOriginError` with `var(--token-name)` references. This is a good place to decide whether to use CSS Modules (`.module.css` files) or keep inline styles but reference tokens. My recommendation:

- Use **inline styles with `var()` references** for simple layout (keeps component files self-contained, matches existing pattern)
- Use **`.module.css` files** only when component-specific pseudo-states (`:hover`, `:focus`, `:active`) are needed (which inline styles cannot do)

For Phase 1, the existing components are too simple to need modules -- they have no interactive states. Just replace the hardcoded hex values with `var()` calls.

---

### Phase 2: Standard Primitives

**Goal**: Build the reusable primitive components that downstream epics need.

**Task 2.1: `Button` component**

The most used primitive. Requirements:
- Variants: `primary` (filled accent), `secondary` (outlined), `ghost` (no border, text only)
- States: default, hover (border intensifies), active (invert colors), focus-visible (focus ring), disabled (grayed out, cursor: not-allowed)
- Props: `variant`, `size` (sm/md/lg), `disabled`, standard button HTML attrs
- Neo-Brutalist style: 2px solid borders, no border-radius, no shadows, uppercase text, JetBrains Mono
- Uses CSS module for pseudo-state styles (`:hover`, `:active`, `:disabled`, `:focus-visible`)
- Keyboard: natively accessible as `<button>` element
- `prefers-reduced-motion`: no transitions when active

Test first (TDD): render variants, verify ARIA states, test keyboard activation, test disabled state suppresses click.

**Task 2.2: `Toggle` switch**

- Binary on/off, renders as `<button role="switch" aria-checked={on}>`
- Visual: rectangular track with square indicator, no rounded corners
- Uses accent color when on, gray when off
- Keyboard: Space to toggle (native with role="switch")

**Task 2.3: `Select` dropdown**

- Start with a styled native `<select>` element wrapped in a div for styling
- Neo-Brutalist: 2px border, no border-radius, custom dropdown arrow (CSS triangle)
- If native `<select>` proves insufficient for downstream epics, upgrade to custom listbox later
- This is the "keep it lean" approach -- native `<select>` is fully accessible by default

**Task 2.4: `Modal` dialog**

- Uses native `<dialog>` element (fully supported in target browsers)
- `showModal()` for backdrop + focus trap (native behavior)
- Props: `open`, `onClose`, `title`, `children`
- Neo-Brutalist: thick black border, no border-radius, white background on black page, visible title bar
- Keyboard: Escape to close (native), Tab trapping (native with `<dialog>`)
- Overlay backdrop: `dialog::backdrop { background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8); }`

**Task 2.5: `Tooltip`**

- Positioned floating element using CSS `position: absolute` on a wrapper
- Shows on hover (`mouseenter`/`mouseleave`) and focus (`focusin`/`focusout`)
- Props: `content`, `placement` (top/bottom/left/right), `children`
- Accessible: `aria-describedby` pointing to tooltip text
- Neo-Brutalist: solid border, no rounded corners, monospace text, black background with white text
- `prefers-reduced-motion`: no fade animation

**Task 2.6: `ContextMenu`**

- Renders on right-click (`contextmenu` event)
- Positioned at mouse coordinates
- Props: `items` (array of `{ label, action, shortcut?, disabled? }`), `children` (trigger)
- Keyboard: arrow keys navigate items, Enter activates, Escape closes
- Accessible: `role="menu"` with `role="menuitem"` children, `aria-haspopup`
- Neo-Brutalist: solid border, monospace, no rounded corners

---

### Phase 3: DAW-Specific Controls

**Goal**: Build the canvas-based and domain-specific controls unique to a DAW.

**Task 3.1: `RotaryKnob`**

The most complex control in the system.

- Canvas-based rendering: draws an arc from min to max value with a pointer line
- Interaction: mousedown starts drag, vertical mouse movement changes value (up = increase, down = decrease)
- Props: `value`, `min`, `max`, `step`, `onChange`, `label`, `size` (px)
- Displays current value as text below the knob (JetBrains Mono)
- Keyboard: focus with Tab, adjust with arrow keys (step), Page Up/Down (10x step), Home/End (min/max)
- Accessible: `role="slider"`, `aria-valuemin`, `aria-valuemax`, `aria-valuenow`, `aria-label`
- Uses `tokens.ts` for canvas colors (cannot use CSS vars in canvas)
- `prefers-reduced-motion`: skip any rotation animations

Test: verify canvas renders (mock `getContext`), verify drag events produce correct values, verify keyboard navigation, verify ARIA attributes.

**Task 3.2: `Fader`**

- Vertical slider control (like a mixing console fader)
- HTML-based (a div track with a draggable thumb), not canvas
- Props: `value`, `min`, `max`, `step`, `onChange`, `label`, `height`
- Keyboard: same as RotaryKnob (arrow keys, Page, Home/End)
- Accessible: `role="slider"`, same ARIA as knob
- Neo-Brutalist: rectangular track, square thumb, 2px borders, accent color for active region
- Could be a styled `<input type="range" orient="vertical">` but custom HTML gives better Neo-Brutalist control

**Task 3.3: `VuMeter`**

- Canvas-based vertical bar meter
- Props: `peakL`, `peakR`, `rmsL?`, `rmsR?`, `clip?` (boolean)
- Renders green-to-yellow-to-red gradient bars based on level
- Peak hold indicator (thin line that decays slowly)
- Clip indicator (red square at top, stays lit until reset)
- Read-only (no interaction), so no keyboard/ARIA needed beyond `role="img"` + `aria-label`
- Uses `requestAnimationFrame` internally for smooth updates (bypasses React state per NFR-13)
- `prefers-reduced-motion`: instant updates, no peak hold animation

**Task 3.4: `DrumPad`**

- A trigger button styled as a square pad
- Props: `label`, `active`, `onTrigger`, `velocity?` (0-1)
- Visual: filled square, lights up briefly on trigger, accent color when active
- Keyboard: Enter or Space to trigger
- Accessible: `<button>` with `aria-label`, `aria-pressed` for toggle mode
- Neo-Brutalist: thick border, no radius, monospace label

**Task 3.5: `StepButton`**

- Toggle button for sequencer steps (16 per row)
- Props: `active`, `currentStep` (highlight when sequencer is on this step), `onToggle`
- Visual: small square, filled with accent color when active, border pulse when currentStep
- Keyboard: Space/Enter to toggle (native `<button>`)
- Accessible: `<button aria-pressed={active}>`

---

### Phase 4: Keyboard Shortcut System

**Goal**: Build the command registry and keyboard listener infrastructure.

**Task 4.1: `command-registry.ts`**

A plain TypeScript class, no React dependencies.

```ts
type Command = {
  id: string;
  label: string;
  execute: () => void;
  enabled?: () => boolean;
};

class CommandRegistry {
  register(command: Command): void;
  unregister(id: string): void;
  execute(id: string): boolean;
  getAll(): readonly Command[];
  get(id: string): Command | undefined;
}
```

Test: register/unregister, execute calls handler, execute returns false for unknown/disabled commands, getAll returns all registered.

**Task 4.2: `shortcut-map.ts`**

Maps keyboard combos to command IDs.

```ts
type ShortcutBinding = {
  key: string;        // e.g., "Space", "KeyM", "KeyZ"
  ctrl?: boolean;
  shift?: boolean;
  alt?: boolean;
  meta?: boolean;
  commandId: string;
};

class ShortcutMap {
  bind(binding: ShortcutBinding): void;
  unbind(key: string, modifiers?: {...}): void;
  resolve(event: KeyboardEvent): string | undefined;  // returns commandId
  getBindingsForCommand(commandId: string): ShortcutBinding[];
  getAll(): readonly ShortcutBinding[];
}
```

Test: bind and resolve, modifier combinations, unbind removes, resolve returns undefined for unbound keys.

**Task 4.3: `useKeyboardShortcuts.ts`**

React hook that wires the registry + shortcut map to `document.addEventListener('keydown', ...)`.

```ts
function useKeyboardShortcuts(
  registry: CommandRegistry,
  shortcuts: ShortcutMap,
): void;
```

- Adds listener on mount, removes on unmount
- Calls `shortcuts.resolve(event)` then `registry.execute(commandId)`
- Ignores events when focus is in `<input>`, `<textarea>`, `<select>`, or `[contenteditable]`
- Does not `preventDefault()` for unhandled keys

Test: verify keydown triggers command, verify ignored in input fields, verify cleanup on unmount.

---

### Phase 5: Accessibility + Reduced Motion

**Goal**: Ensure WCAG 2.2 AA compliance and reduced-motion support are baked in.

**Task 5.1: `useReducedMotion.ts`**

```ts
function useReducedMotion(): boolean;
```

Uses `window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)')`. Returns `true` when user prefers reduced motion. Updates on media query change.

Test: mock `matchMedia`, verify initial value, verify change listener.

**Task 5.2: Focus management audit**

- All interactive components must have `:focus-visible` styles (handled by `global.css` baseline)
- Modal must trap focus (native `<dialog>` handles this)
- ContextMenu must return focus to trigger on close
- All custom controls (`RotaryKnob`, `Fader`) must accept Tab focus and support keyboard operation

This is not a separate code deliverable but a **verification pass** across all Phase 2 and 3 components. Each component's test file should include a keyboard navigation test.

**Task 5.3: Update barrel export `src/ui/index.ts`**

Add all new exports:
- All primitives: `Button`, `Select`, `Modal`, `Tooltip`, `ContextMenu`, `Toggle`
- All controls: `RotaryKnob`, `Fader`, `VuMeter`, `DrumPad`, `StepButton`
- Types: `ThemeTokens` from `tokens.ts`
- System: `CommandRegistry` from `command-registry.ts`, `ShortcutMap` from `shortcut-map.ts`, `useKeyboardShortcuts`, `useReducedMotion`

---

### Phase Ordering and Dependencies

```
Phase 1 (Tokens + Global CSS)     -- no dependencies, foundation for everything
  |
  +-- Phase 2 (Primitives)        -- depends on tokens
  |     |
  |     +-- Phase 3 (Controls)    -- depends on tokens; some controls may use Button internally
  |
  +-- Phase 4 (Keyboard System)   -- depends on nothing except TS; pure logic
  |
  +-- Phase 5 (A11y + Exports)    -- depends on all above being complete
```

Phases 2, 3, and 4 can be worked on in parallel once Phase 1 is done. Phase 5 is a finalization pass.

---

### Key Architectural Decisions

1. **CSS custom properties (not CSS-in-JS)**: Per the constraints. Tokens live in one `.css` file imported at the root. All components reference `var(--token-name)`. Canvas components use the mirrored `tokens.ts` constants.

2. **CSS Modules for components with pseudo-states**: Buttons, toggles, and other interactive elements need `:hover`, `:active`, `:disabled` styles that inline styles cannot express. CSS Modules (`Component.module.css`) provide scoped class names with zero runtime cost. Vite supports them out of the box.

3. **Native HTML elements where possible**: `<button>` for Button/DrumPad/StepButton/Toggle, `<dialog>` for Modal, `<select>` for Select. This gives us accessibility for free and reduces custom ARIA work.

4. **Canvas for high-frequency rendering only**: RotaryKnob (arc drawing) and VuMeter (60fps updates) justify canvas. Fader, DrumPad, and StepButton are simpler DOM elements.

5. **Command registry is framework-agnostic**: `CommandRegistry` and `ShortcutMap` are pure TypeScript classes with no React imports. Only `useKeyboardShortcuts` is a React hook. This allows the registry to be used by Zustand actions or audio engine code later.

6. **No font bundling**: Load JetBrains Mono via Google Fonts CDN link in `index.html`. Bundling the font adds complexity for no benefit in a web app.

7. **Minimum viable for downstream epics**: The controls are designed to match exactly what E3 (Transport), E6 (Mixer), E7 (Synth), E8 (808), E9 (Effects), E10 (Piano Roll), E11 (Arrangement) will need. No speculative components.

---

### Potential Challenges

1. **Canvas testing in jsdom**: jsdom does not implement `CanvasRenderingContext2D`. Tests for `RotaryKnob` and `VuMeter` will need to mock `HTMLCanvasElement.prototype.getContext`. The mock should verify that draw calls are made with correct parameters (colors from tokens, coordinates from value calculations). Consider a helper in `src/test/` for canvas mocking.

2. **`exactOptionalPropertyTypes` with component props**: This tsconfig option means optional props cannot be explicitly set to `undefined`. Component prop types must use `prop?: T` carefully and avoid patterns like `{ value: number | undefined }`. This is already enforced project-wide.

3. **`noUncheckedIndexedAccess` with shortcut map lookups**: The `ShortcutMap.resolve()` method will involve map lookups that return `T | undefined`. Code must handle this explicitly rather than assuming keys exist.

4. **CSS custom property support in tests**: jsdom does not fully support `getComputedStyle` reading CSS custom properties. Tests should focus on structural output (correct classnames, ARIA attributes, event handling) rather than computed visual styles.

5. **Reduced motion in tests**: Need to mock `window.matchMedia` in test setup. This should be added to `src/test/setup.ts` as a global mock.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/tokens/tokens.css` -- The single source of truth for all design tokens (colors, spacing, typography, borders). Every other component depends on this file.
- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/tokens/tokens.ts` -- TypeScript mirror of tokens for canvas-based components and type exports (`ThemeTokens`).
- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/keyboard/command-registry.ts` -- The command registry that powers the entire keyboard shortcut system and will be used by transport, mixer, and every interactive epic downstream.
- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/RotaryKnob.tsx` -- The most complex custom control (canvas + drag + keyboard + ARIA), representative of the DAW-specific component pattern.
- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/global.css` -- CSS reset, font loading, focus ring defaults, and reduced-motion media query -- the foundation that makes the token system work in the browser.

tool_use_result.prompt:
Plan the implementation of E2: Neo-Brutalist Design System for a browser-based DAW called BRUTALWAV.

## Current State
- React 19 + TypeScript strict + Vite + Zustand + Vitest
- Path aliases: @ui, @audio, @state, @
- Existing UI: DawShell, Toolbar, panels (Arrangement, Mixer, Instrument), ClickToStart, CrossOriginError
- All inline styles currently, no CSS system yet
- JetBrains Mono already referenced in components
- 52 tests passing

## Epic Requirements (from beads description)
Custom design system from scratch (NOT shadcn/ui):
1. Design tokens: color palette (true black #000, true white #FFF, electric blue, hot pink, acid green, amber, pure red), JetBrains Mono for data/values, system neo-grotesque for headings, 8px spatial grid
2. Custom components: rotary knobs (canvas-based with drag), vertical faders/sliders, VU/peak meters (canvas), drum pads, step buttons, toggle switches
3. Standard primitives: buttons (hover/active/focus/disabled states), dropdowns, modals, tooltips, context menus
4. Keyboard shortcut system (command registry + shortcut map)
5. Focus management and WCAG 2.2 AA compliance
6. prefers-reduced-motion support (NFR-05)
7. Neo-Brutalist aesthetic: visible structural borders, exposed grid, high-contrast, monospace data display

EARS requirements referenced: R-UBI-06 (8px grid), R-UBI-09 (Neo-Brutalist system), R-EVT-17 (keyboard shortcuts), NFR-04 (keyboard navigation), NFR-05 (reduced motion)

Interface exports: all UI components, ThemeTokens, CommandRegistry

## Constraints
- TDD: tests first, then implement
- TypeScript strict with noUncheckedIndexedAccess
- ESLint strict type-checked
- No shadcn/ui, no component library - custom from scratch
- Keep it lean - minimum viable for downstream epics (E3, E6-E11 etc.)
- Use CSS custom properties for tokens (not CSS-in-JS)

## Project Structure
Files should go under src/ui/ with appropriate subdirectories. Think about the right module structure.

Please create a detailed implementation plan with phases, file structure, and task ordering. Focus on what's actually needed - don't over-engineer.

content.text:
I now have a thorough understanding of every relevant piece of the codebase. Let me synthesize the implementation plan.

---

## E2: Neo-Brutalist Design System -- Implementation Plan

### Context Summary

The current codebase (E1) has:
- 6 UI components (`DawShell`, `Toolbar`, `panels` x3, `ClickToStart`, `CrossOriginError`) all using inline React `style={}` objects
- No CSS files whatsoever in `src/`
- JetBrains Mono referenced as a string in inline styles but never loaded via `@font-face` or link
- Hardcoded hex colors throughout (`#000`, `#111`, `#333`, `#666`, `#888`, `#fff`, `#ff3333`)
- 8px-ish spacing used inconsistently (some 16px, some 32px, some 48px)
- No keyboard shortcut system
- No focus styles
- 52 tests passing, all using `@testing-library/react` with `data-testid` and semantic queries
- Zustand store at `src/state/store.ts` with transport + engine status
- Path aliases: `@ui/*` resolves to `src/ui/*`

The spec calls for a **custom** design system (explicitly "NOT shadcn/ui"), using **CSS custom properties** for tokens (not CSS-in-JS). The advisory brief flagged this as high-effort (P1-14) but it was deliberately overridden in the epic requirements.

---

### File Structure

All new files under `src/ui/` with this layout:

```
src/ui/
  tokens/
    tokens.css              -- CSS custom properties (the single source of truth)
    tokens.ts               -- TypeScript constants mirroring CSS tokens for canvas/JS use
    tokens.test.ts          -- Validate TS constants match CSS values; test token completeness
  primitives/
    Button.tsx              -- <button> with neo-brutalist states
    Button.test.tsx
    Select.tsx              -- Custom dropdown (styled native <select> or custom listbox)
    Select.test.tsx
    Modal.tsx               -- Dialog with backdrop, trap focus
    Modal.test.tsx
    Tooltip.tsx             -- Positioned tooltip on hover/focus
    Tooltip.test.tsx
    ContextMenu.tsx         -- Right-click context menu
    ContextMenu.test.tsx
    Toggle.tsx              -- On/off toggle switch
    Toggle.test.tsx
  controls/
    RotaryKnob.tsx          -- Canvas-based rotary control with drag
    RotaryKnob.test.tsx
    Fader.tsx               -- Vertical fader/slider
    Fader.test.tsx
    VuMeter.tsx             -- Canvas-based VU/peak meter
    VuMeter.test.tsx
    DrumPad.tsx             -- Trigger pad (button with velocity)
    DrumPad.test.tsx
    StepButton.tsx          -- Sequencer step toggle
    StepButton.test.tsx
  keyboard/
    command-registry.ts     -- CommandRegistry class
    command-registry.test.ts
    shortcut-map.ts         -- Default shortcut bindings
    shortcut-map.test.ts
    useKeyboardShortcuts.ts -- React hook for global listener
    useKeyboardShortcuts.test.ts
  hooks/
    useReducedMotion.ts     -- prefers-reduced-motion media query hook
    useReducedMotion.test.ts
  global.css                -- Reset, font loading, body defaults, reduced-motion
  index.ts                  -- Re-export barrel (updated)
```

---

### Phase 1: Tokens + Global CSS Foundation

**Goal**: Establish the design token layer that everything else builds on. Zero runtime, pure CSS custom properties.

**Task 1.1: `src/ui/tokens/tokens.css`**

Define all CSS custom properties on `:root`:

- **Colors**: `--color-black: #000`, `--color-white: #FFF`, `--color-blue: #0066FF` (electric blue), `--color-pink: #FF2D6F` (hot pink), `--color-green: #39FF14` (acid green), `--color-amber: #FFB800`, `--color-red: #FF0000` (pure red). Plus grays: `--color-gray-900: #111`, `--color-gray-700: #333`, `--color-gray-500: #666`, `--color-gray-300: #999`, `--color-gray-100: #E5E5E5`.
- **Typography**: `--font-mono: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace`, `--font-sans: system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif`. Size scale on 8px grid: `--text-xs: 10px`, `--text-sm: 12px`, `--text-base: 14px`, `--text-lg: 16px`, `--text-xl: 20px`, `--text-2xl: 24px`, `--text-3xl: 32px`, `--text-4xl: 48px`.
- **Spacing**: `--space-1: 4px`, `--space-2: 8px`, `--space-3: 12px`, `--space-4: 16px`, `--space-6: 24px`, `--space-8: 32px`, `--space-12: 48px`, `--space-16: 64px` (8px grid, with 4px half-step).
- **Borders**: `--border-width: 2px`, `--border-color: var(--color-gray-700)`, `--border: var(--border-width) solid var(--border-color)`.
- **Shadows**: None (Neo-Brutalist rejects drop shadows).
- **Focus ring**: `--focus-ring: 2px solid var(--color-blue)`, `--focus-offset: 2px`.
- **Transitions**: `--transition-fast: 100ms`, `--transition-normal: 200ms` (used only when reduced-motion is not active).

Note on exact hex values: The spec says "electric blue, hot pink, acid green, amber, pure red" but does not prescribe exact hex values except for `#000` and `#FFF`. The values above are chosen for WCAG AA contrast against black backgrounds (the primary ground). These should be validated during implementation -- particularly `--color-green` which at `#39FF14` has excellent contrast on black (>7:1) but poor contrast on white. Since this is a dark-ground-dominant DAW, that's acceptable.

**Task 1.2: `src/ui/tokens/tokens.ts`**

TypeScript constants object that mirrors the CSS tokens. This is needed because canvas-based components (`RotaryKnob`, `VuMeter`) cannot read CSS custom properties during `CanvasRenderingContext2D` draw calls -- they need raw JS values. Export as a `const` assertion (`as const`) for maximum type safety.

```ts
export const tokens = {
  color: {
    black: "#000000",
    white: "#FFFFFF",
    blue: "#0066FF",
    // ...
  },
  space: {
    1: 4,
    2: 8,
    // ...  (numeric values in px for canvas math)
  },
  font: {
    mono: "'JetBrains Mono', monospace",
    sans: "system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif",
  },
} as const;

export type ThemeTokens = typeof tokens;
```

**Task 1.3: `src/ui/tokens/tokens.test.ts`**

Test that the TypeScript tokens object has all expected categories and values. Validate that every color is a valid hex string, every spacing value is a multiple of 4, font strings are non-empty. This is a "contract test" ensuring the TS mirror does not drift from the CSS.

**Task 1.4: `src/ui/global.css`**

- CSS reset (minimal: `*, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }`)
- `@font-face` for JetBrains Mono (or Google Fonts `@import` -- check if the font is already available; the existing inline styles reference it by name but never load it)
- `body` defaults: `background: var(--color-black); color: var(--color-white); font-family: var(--font-mono);`
- `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block that sets `--transition-fast: 0ms; --transition-normal: 0ms;` and `* { animation-duration: 0ms !important; transition-duration: 0ms !important; }`
- Focus visible styles: `:focus-visible { outline: var(--focus-ring); outline-offset: var(--focus-offset); }`

**Task 1.5: Wire CSS into the app**

- Import `src/ui/tokens/tokens.css` and `src/ui/global.css` in `src/main.tsx` (the entry point). Vite handles CSS imports.
- Add JetBrains Mono font link to `index.html` `<head>` (Google Fonts CDN is the simplest path)
- Update `index.html` `<title>` to "BRUTALWAV"

**Task 1.6: Migrate existing components from inline styles to CSS tokens**

Replace hardcoded values in `DawShell`, `Toolbar`, `panels`, `ClickToStart`, `CrossOriginError` with `var(--token-name)` references. This is a good place to decide whether to use CSS Modules (`.module.css` files) or keep inline styles but reference tokens. My recommendation:

- Use **inline styles with `var()` references** for simple layout (keeps component files self-contained, matches existing pattern)
- Use **`.module.css` files** only when component-specific pseudo-states (`:hover`, `:focus`, `:active`) are needed (which inline styles cannot do)

For Phase 1, the existing components are too simple to need modules -- they have no interactive states. Just replace the hardcoded hex values with `var()` calls.

---

### Phase 2: Standard Primitives

**Goal**: Build the reusable primitive components that downstream epics need.

**Task 2.1: `Button` component**

The most used primitive. Requirements:
- Variants: `primary` (filled accent), `secondary` (outlined), `ghost` (no border, text only)
- States: default, hover (border intensifies), active (invert colors), focus-visible (focus ring), disabled (grayed out, cursor: not-allowed)
- Props: `variant`, `size` (sm/md/lg), `disabled`, standard button HTML attrs
- Neo-Brutalist style: 2px solid borders, no border-radius, no shadows, uppercase text, JetBrains Mono
- Uses CSS module for pseudo-state styles (`:hover`, `:active`, `:disabled`, `:focus-visible`)
- Keyboard: natively accessible as `<button>` element
- `prefers-reduced-motion`: no transitions when active

Test first (TDD): render variants, verify ARIA states, test keyboard activation, test disabled state suppresses click.

**Task 2.2: `Toggle` switch**

- Binary on/off, renders as `<button role="switch" aria-checked={on}>`
- Visual: rectangular track with square indicator, no rounded corners
- Uses accent color when on, gray when off
- Keyboard: Space to toggle (native with role="switch")

**Task 2.3: `Select` dropdown**

- Start with a styled native `<select>` element wrapped in a div for styling
- Neo-Brutalist: 2px border, no border-radius, custom dropdown arrow (CSS triangle)
- If native `<select>` proves insufficient for downstream epics, upgrade to custom listbox later
- This is the "keep it lean" approach -- native `<select>` is fully accessible by default

**Task 2.4: `Modal` dialog**

- Uses native `<dialog>` element (fully supported in target browsers)
- `showModal()` for backdrop + focus trap (native behavior)
- Props: `open`, `onClose`, `title`, `children`
- Neo-Brutalist: thick black border, no border-radius, white background on black page, visible title bar
- Keyboard: Escape to close (native), Tab trapping (native with `<dialog>`)
- Overlay backdrop: `dialog::backdrop { background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8); }`

**Task 2.5: `Tooltip`**

- Positioned floating element using CSS `position: absolute` on a wrapper
- Shows on hover (`mouseenter`/`mouseleave`) and focus (`focusin`/`focusout`)
- Props: `content`, `placement` (top/bottom/left/right), `children`
- Accessible: `aria-describedby` pointing to tooltip text
- Neo-Brutalist: solid border, no rounded corners, monospace text, black background with white text
- `prefers-reduced-motion`: no fade animation

**Task 2.6: `ContextMenu`**

- Renders on right-click (`contextmenu` event)
- Positioned at mouse coordinates
- Props: `items` (array of `{ label, action, shortcut?, disabled? }`), `children` (trigger)
- Keyboard: arrow keys navigate items, Enter activates, Escape closes
- Accessible: `role="menu"` with `role="menuitem"` children, `aria-haspopup`
- Neo-Brutalist: solid border, monospace, no rounded corners

---

### Phase 3: DAW-Specific Controls

**Goal**: Build the canvas-based and domain-specific controls unique to a DAW.

**Task 3.1: `RotaryKnob`**

The most complex control in the system.

- Canvas-based rendering: draws an arc from min to max value with a pointer line
- Interaction: mousedown starts drag, vertical mouse movement changes value (up = increase, down = decrease)
- Props: `value`, `min`, `max`, `step`, `onChange`, `label`, `size` (px)
- Displays current value as text below the knob (JetBrains Mono)
- Keyboard: focus with Tab, adjust with arrow keys (step), Page Up/Down (10x step), Home/End (min/max)
- Accessible: `role="slider"`, `aria-valuemin`, `aria-valuemax`, `aria-valuenow`, `aria-label`
- Uses `tokens.ts` for canvas colors (cannot use CSS vars in canvas)
- `prefers-reduced-motion`: skip any rotation animations

Test: verify canvas renders (mock `getContext`), verify drag events produce correct values, verify keyboard navigation, verify ARIA attributes.

**Task 3.2: `Fader`**

- Vertical slider control (like a mixing console fader)
- HTML-based (a div track with a draggable thumb), not canvas
- Props: `value`, `min`, `max`, `step`, `onChange`, `label`, `height`
- Keyboard: same as RotaryKnob (arrow keys, Page, Home/End)
- Accessible: `role="slider"`, same ARIA as knob
- Neo-Brutalist: rectangular track, square thumb, 2px borders, accent color for active region
- Could be a styled `<input type="range" orient="vertical">` but custom HTML gives better Neo-Brutalist control

**Task 3.3: `VuMeter`**

- Canvas-based vertical bar meter
- Props: `peakL`, `peakR`, `rmsL?`, `rmsR?`, `clip?` (boolean)
- Renders green-to-yellow-to-red gradient bars based on level
- Peak hold indicator (thin line that decays slowly)
- Clip indicator (red square at top, stays lit until reset)
- Read-only (no interaction), so no keyboard/ARIA needed beyond `role="img"` + `aria-label`
- Uses `requestAnimationFrame` internally for smooth updates (bypasses React state per NFR-13)
- `prefers-reduced-motion`: instant updates, no peak hold animation

**Task 3.4: `DrumPad`**

- A trigger button styled as a square pad
- Props: `label`, `active`, `onTrigger`, `velocity?` (0-1)
- Visual: filled square, lights up briefly on trigger, accent color when active
- Keyboard: Enter or Space to trigger
- Accessible: `<button>` with `aria-label`, `aria-pressed` for toggle mode
- Neo-Brutalist: thick border, no radius, monospace label

**Task 3.5: `StepButton`**

- Toggle button for sequencer steps (16 per row)
- Props: `active`, `currentStep` (highlight when sequencer is on this step), `onToggle`
- Visual: small square, filled with accent color when active, border pulse when currentStep
- Keyboard: Space/Enter to toggle (native `<button>`)
- Accessible: `<button aria-pressed={active}>`

---

### Phase 4: Keyboard Shortcut System

**Goal**: Build the command registry and keyboard listener infrastructure.

**Task 4.1: `command-registry.ts`**

A plain TypeScript class, no React dependencies.

```ts
type Command = {
  id: string;
  label: string;
  execute: () => void;
  enabled?: () => boolean;
};

class CommandRegistry {
  register(command: Command): void;
  unregister(id: string): void;
  execute(id: string): boolean;
  getAll(): readonly Command[];
  get(id: string): Command | undefined;
}
```

Test: register/unregister, execute calls handler, execute returns false for unknown/disabled commands, getAll returns all registered.

**Task 4.2: `shortcut-map.ts`**

Maps keyboard combos to command IDs.

```ts
type ShortcutBinding = {
  key: string;        // e.g., "Space", "KeyM", "KeyZ"
  ctrl?: boolean;
  shift?: boolean;
  alt?: boolean;
  meta?: boolean;
  commandId: string;
};

class ShortcutMap {
  bind(binding: ShortcutBinding): void;
  unbind(key: string, modifiers?: {...}): void;
  resolve(event: KeyboardEvent): string | undefined;  // returns commandId
  getBindingsForCommand(commandId: string): ShortcutBinding[];
  getAll(): readonly ShortcutBinding[];
}
```

Test: bind and resolve, modifier combinations, unbind removes, resolve returns undefined for unbound keys.

**Task 4.3: `useKeyboardShortcuts.ts`**

React hook that wires the registry + shortcut map to `document.addEventListener('keydown', ...)`.

```ts
function useKeyboardShortcuts(
  registry: CommandRegistry,
  shortcuts: ShortcutMap,
): void;
```

- Adds listener on mount, removes on unmount
- Calls `shortcuts.resolve(event)` then `registry.execute(commandId)`
- Ignores events when focus is in `<input>`, `<textarea>`, `<select>`, or `[contenteditable]`
- Does not `preventDefault()` for unhandled keys

Test: verify keydown triggers command, verify ignored in input fields, verify cleanup on unmount.

---

### Phase 5: Accessibility + Reduced Motion

**Goal**: Ensure WCAG 2.2 AA compliance and reduced-motion support are baked in.

**Task 5.1: `useReducedMotion.ts`**

```ts
function useReducedMotion(): boolean;
```

Uses `window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)')`. Returns `true` when user prefers reduced motion. Updates on media query change.

Test: mock `matchMedia`, verify initial value, verify change listener.

**Task 5.2: Focus management audit**

- All interactive components must have `:focus-visible` styles (handled by `global.css` baseline)
- Modal must trap focus (native `<dialog>` handles this)
- ContextMenu must return focus to trigger on close
- All custom controls (`RotaryKnob`, `Fader`) must accept Tab focus and support keyboard operation

This is not a separate code deliverable but a **verification pass** across all Phase 2 and 3 components. Each component's test file should include a keyboard navigation test.

**Task 5.3: Update barrel export `src/ui/index.ts`**

Add all new exports:
- All primitives: `Button`, `Select`, `Modal`, `Tooltip`, `ContextMenu`, `Toggle`
- All controls: `RotaryKnob`, `Fader`, `VuMeter`, `DrumPad`, `StepButton`
- Types: `ThemeTokens` from `tokens.ts`
- System: `CommandRegistry` from `command-registry.ts`, `ShortcutMap` from `shortcut-map.ts`, `useKeyboardShortcuts`, `useReducedMotion`

---

### Phase Ordering and Dependencies

```
Phase 1 (Tokens + Global CSS)     -- no dependencies, foundation for everything
  |
  +-- Phase 2 (Primitives)        -- depends on tokens
  |     |
  |     +-- Phase 3 (Controls)    -- depends on tokens; some controls may use Button internally
  |
  +-- Phase 4 (Keyboard System)   -- depends on nothing except TS; pure logic
  |
  +-- Phase 5 (A11y + Exports)    -- depends on all above being complete
```

Phases 2, 3, and 4 can be worked on in parallel once Phase 1 is done. Phase 5 is a finalization pass.

---

### Key Architectural Decisions

1. **CSS custom properties (not CSS-in-JS)**: Per the constraints. Tokens live in one `.css` file imported at the root. All components reference `var(--token-name)`. Canvas components use the mirrored `tokens.ts` constants.

2. **CSS Modules for components with pseudo-states**: Buttons, toggles, and other interactive elements need `:hover`, `:active`, `:disabled` styles that inline styles cannot express. CSS Modules (`Component.module.css`) provide scoped class names with zero runtime cost. Vite supports them out of the box.

3. **Native HTML elements where possible**: `<button>` for Button/DrumPad/StepButton/Toggle, `<dialog>` for Modal, `<select>` for Select. This gives us accessibility for free and reduces custom ARIA work.

4. **Canvas for high-frequency rendering only**: RotaryKnob (arc drawing) and VuMeter (60fps updates) justify canvas. Fader, DrumPad, and StepButton are simpler DOM elements.

5. **Command registry is framework-agnostic**: `CommandRegistry` and `ShortcutMap` are pure TypeScript classes with no React imports. Only `useKeyboardShortcuts` is a React hook. This allows the registry to be used by Zustand actions or audio engine code later.

6. **No font bundling**: Load JetBrains Mono via Google Fonts CDN link in `index.html`. Bundling the font adds complexity for no benefit in a web app.

7. **Minimum viable for downstream epics**: The controls are designed to match exactly what E3 (Transport), E6 (Mixer), E7 (Synth), E8 (808), E9 (Effects), E10 (Piano Roll), E11 (Arrangement) will need. No speculative components.

---

### Potential Challenges

1. **Canvas testing in jsdom**: jsdom does not implement `CanvasRenderingContext2D`. Tests for `RotaryKnob` and `VuMeter` will need to mock `HTMLCanvasElement.prototype.getContext`. The mock should verify that draw calls are made with correct parameters (colors from tokens, coordinates from value calculations). Consider a helper in `src/test/` for canvas mocking.

2. **`exactOptionalPropertyTypes` with component props**: This tsconfig option means optional props cannot be explicitly set to `undefined`. Component prop types must use `prop?: T` carefully and avoid patterns like `{ value: number | undefined }`. This is already enforced project-wide.

3. **`noUncheckedIndexedAccess` with shortcut map lookups**: The `ShortcutMap.resolve()` method will involve map lookups that return `T | undefined`. Code must handle this explicitly rather than assuming keys exist.

4. **CSS custom property support in tests**: jsdom does not fully support `getComputedStyle` reading CSS custom properties. Tests should focus on structural output (correct classnames, ARIA attributes, event handling) rather than computed visual styles.

5. **Reduced motion in tests**: Need to mock `window.matchMedia` in test setup. This should be added to `src/test/setup.ts` as a global mock.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/tokens/tokens.css` -- The single source of truth for all design tokens (colors, spacing, typography, borders). Every other component depends on this file.
- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/tokens/tokens.ts` -- TypeScript mirror of tokens for canvas-based components and type exports (`ThemeTokens`).
- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/keyboard/command-registry.ts` -- The command registry that powers the entire keyboard shortcut system and will be used by transport, mixer, and every interactive epic downstream.
- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/RotaryKnob.tsx` -- The most complex custom control (canvas + drag + keyboard + ARIA), representative of the DAW-specific component pattern.
- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/global.css` -- CSS reset, font loading, focus ring defaults, and reduced-motion media query -- the foundation that makes the token system work in the browser.

Implement a Button component for a Neo-Brutalist DAW design system in /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/.

Tech: React 19, TypeScript strict (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes), Vitest, @testing-library/react.

Follow TDD: write tests FIRST in Button.test.tsx, then implement Button.tsx + Button.module.css.

## Button Spec
- Variants: `primary` (filled with accent blue), `secondary` (outlined), `ghost` (no border, text only)
- Sizes: `sm` (24px height), `md` (32px height), `lg` (40px height)
- States: hover, active, focus-visible, disabled
- Props: `variant?: "primary" | "secondary" | "ghost"`, `size?: "sm" | "md" | "lg"`, plus all native button HTML attrs
- Defaults: variant="secondary", size="md"
- Neo-Brutalist style: 2px solid borders, NO border-radius, NO shadows, uppercase text, JetBrains Mono font
- Must use CSS module for pseudo-state styles
- Must render a native `<button>` element
- Keyboard: natively accessible
- Use var(--token) CSS custom properties from the token system (color-black, color-white, color-blue, color-gray-700, color-gray-500, font-mono, text-sm, text-base, border-width, space-2, space-4, transition-fast)

## Test Requirements
Test in Button.test.tsx:
1. Renders with default variant/size
2. Renders each variant correctly (className check)
3. Renders each size
4. Calls onClick handler
5. Does NOT call onClick when disabled
6. Has correct aria-disabled when disabled
7. Forwards refs and additional HTML button props

## Important Rules
- File path: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Button.tsx
- CSS module: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Button.module.css
- Test: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Button.test.tsx
- Use `import styles from "./Button.module.css"` for CSS modules
- Export `Button` as named export
- eslint requires explicit return types on functions: `function Button(...): React.JSX.Element`
- For the props type, use intersection with Omit<React.ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>, 'disabled'> to include native props
- No emojis in code or comments
- Run tests with: cd /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness && pnpm vitest run src/ui/primitives/Button.test.tsx
- If tests fail, fix them

Tech: React 19, TypeScript strict (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes), Vitest, @testing-library/react.

Follow TDD: write tests FIRST in Toggle.test.tsx, then implement Toggle.tsx + Toggle.module.css.

## Toggle Spec
- Binary on/off switch
- Renders as `<button role="switch" aria-checked={on}>`
- Props: `checked: boolean`, `onChange: (checked: boolean) => void`, `label: string`, `disabled?: boolean`
- Visual: rectangular track with square indicator, NO rounded corners
- Uses accent color (blue) when on, gray-700 when off
- Track size: 40px wide, 20px tall; thumb 16px square
- Keyboard: Space to toggle (native button behavior)
- Neo-Brutalist: 2px solid border, no rounded corners

## Test Requirements
1. Renders with aria-checked="false" when off
2. Renders with aria-checked="true" when on
3. Calls onChange with toggled value on click
4. Does NOT call onChange when disabled
5. Has accessible label (aria-label)
6. Has role="switch"

## Important Rules
- File: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Toggle.tsx
- CSS: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Toggle.module.css
- Test: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Toggle.test.tsx
- Named export `Toggle`
- eslint: explicit return types

Implement a Modal dialog component for a Neo-Brutalist DAW design system in /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/.

Tech: React 19, TypeScript strict (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes), Vitest, @testing-library/react.

Follow TDD: write tests FIRST in Modal.test.tsx, then implement Modal.tsx + Modal.module.css.

## Modal Spec
- Uses native `<dialog>` element
- Props: `open: boolean`, `onClose: () => void`, `title: string`, `children: React.ReactNode`
- Shows via `dialog.showModal()` when open is true (useEffect + useRef)
- Closes on Escape (native dialog behavior) - listen for 'close' event to call onClose
- Neo-Brutalist: thick 2px black border ON the dialog itself, no border-radius, dark background (gray-900), visible title bar with border-bottom, monospace font
- Backdrop: `dialog::backdrop { background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.85); }`
- Focus trap is native with `<dialog>`

## Test Requirements
1. Renders dialog element (use `getByRole("dialog")` when open)
2. Shows title text
3. Shows children content
4. Calls onClose when close event fires
5. Dialog is not visible when open=false

IMPORTANT: jsdom does NOT implement showModal() or the dialog element fully. You must mock it:
```ts
// In the test file, before the describe block:
beforeAll(() => {
  HTMLDialogElement.prototype.showModal = vi.fn();
  HTMLDialogElement.prototype.close = vi.fn();
});
```

Also, when open=true the dialog should have the `open` attribute set. Since jsdom's showModal is mocked, set the attribute manually in the useEffect after calling showModal.

## Important Rules
- File: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Modal.tsx
- CSS: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Modal.module.css
- Test: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Modal.test.tsx
- Named export `Modal`
- eslint requires explicit return types
- Use var(--color-*), var(--font-mono), var(--border), var(--text-lg), var(--space-4)
- No emojis
- Run tests: cd /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness && pnpm vitest run src/ui/primitives/Modal.test.tsx

input.prompt:
Implement a Tooltip component for a Neo-Brutalist DAW design system in /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/.

Tech: React 19, TypeScript strict (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes), Vitest, @testing-library/react.

Follow TDD: write tests FIRST in Tooltip.test.tsx, then implement Tooltip.tsx + Tooltip.module.css.

## Tooltip Spec
- Positioned floating element using CSS position: absolute on a wrapper
- Shows on mouseenter/focus, hides on mouseleave/blur
- Props: `content: string`, `placement?: "top" | "bottom" | "left" | "right"` (default "top"), `children: React.ReactElement`
- Accessible: uses aria-describedby on the children element, tooltip has role="tooltip"
- Neo-Brutalist: solid 2px border, no rounded corners, monospace text, black background with white text, small padding
- prefers-reduced-motion: no fade animation (CSS handles this via transition-fast token)

## Test Requirements
1. Does not show tooltip content by default
2. Shows tooltip on mouseenter of trigger element
3. Hides tooltip on mouseleave
4. Tooltip has role="tooltip"
5. Trigger has aria-describedby pointing to tooltip id

Implementation note: wrap children in a span to attach event handlers. Use `React.useId()` for generating unique tooltip IDs. The tooltip span should be conditionally rendered or use visibility/opacity.

## Important Rules
- File: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Tooltip.tsx
- CSS: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Tooltip.module.css
- Test: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Tooltip.test.tsx
- Named export `Tooltip`
- eslint requires explicit return types
- Use var(--color-*), var(--font-mono), var(--border), var(--text-xs), var(--space-1), var(--space-2), var(--transition-fast)
- No emojis
- Run tests: cd /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness && pnpm vitest run src/ui/primitives/Tooltip.test.tsx

Implement a ContextMenu component for a Neo-Brutalist DAW design system in /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/.

Tech: React 19, TypeScript strict (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes), Vitest, @testing-library/react.

Follow TDD: write tests FIRST in ContextMenu.test.tsx, then implement ContextMenu.tsx + ContextMenu.module.css.

## ContextMenu Spec
- Renders on right-click (contextmenu event) of children
- Positioned at mouse coordinates using position: fixed
- Props:
  ```ts
  type MenuItem = {
    label: string;
    action: () => void;
    shortcut?: string;
    disabled?: boolean;
  };
  type ContextMenuProps = {
    items: MenuItem[];
    children: React.ReactNode;
  };
  ```
- Keyboard: arrow keys navigate items, Enter activates focused item, Escape closes
- Accessible: role="menu" on the menu, role="menuitem" on items, aria-disabled for disabled items
- Closes when clicking outside or pressing Escape
- Neo-Brutalist: solid 2px border, no rounded corners, monospace text, dark background (gray-900), items show shortcut text right-aligned if provided

## Test Requirements
1. Does not show menu by default
2. Shows menu on contextmenu event (right-click)
3. Menu has role="menu"
4. Items have role="menuitem"
5. Calls item action on click
6. Closes on Escape key
7. Disabled items don't call action

## Important Rules
- File: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/ContextMenu.tsx
- CSS: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/ContextMenu.module.css
- Test: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/ContextMenu.test.tsx
- Named export `ContextMenu` and `MenuItem` type
- eslint requires explicit return types
- Use var(--color-*), var(--font-mono), var(--border), var(--text-sm), var(--space-2), var(--space-4)
- No emojis
- For the contextmenu event test, use `fireEvent.contextMenu(trigger)` from testing-library
- For outside click, add a document click listener that closes the menu
- Run tests: cd /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness && pnpm vitest run src/ui/primitives/ContextMenu.test.tsx

- States: hover, active, focus-visible, disabled
- Props: `variant?: "primary" | "secondary" | "ghost"`, `size?: "sm" | "md" | "lg"`, plus all native button HTML attrs

TypeScript and ESLint both pass clean. Here is a summary of what was implemented:

---

**Files created:**

- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Select.test.tsx` -- 6 tests covering all requirements (native select render, options display, onChange callback, label text, disabled state, aria-label)
- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Select.tsx` -- Named export `Select` component wrapping a native `<select>` in a Neo-Brutalist styled container
- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Select.module.css` -- Styling with CSS custom properties (`--color-*`, `--font-mono`, `--border`, `--text-sm`, `--space-2`), no border-radius, 2px solid border, black background, white text, "V" character as dropdown arrow

**Design decisions:**

- Used CSS Modules (`Select.module.css`) consistent with the project's CSS approach
- Bracket notation for `styles["container"]` etc. to satisfy `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` (avoids `string | undefined` type issues)
- `disabled` prop typed as `boolean | undefined` (optional) -- works with `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` since the native `disabled` attribute accepts `boolean | undefined`
- Label rendered as a `<span>` above the select, with the native `aria-label` on the `<select>` for accessibility
- Installed `@testing-library/user-event` as a dev dependency for interaction testing

**Files created:**

- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Toggle.test.tsx` -- 8 tests covering all spec requirements (role, aria-checked states, onChange toggling, disabled behavior, accessible label)
- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Toggle.tsx` -- Named export `Toggle` component rendering a `<button role="switch">` with proper aria attributes, disabled support, and CSS module classes
- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Toggle.module.css` -- Neo-Brutalist styling: 40x20px rectangular track, 16px square thumb, 2px solid border, no border-radius, uses CSS custom properties (`--color-blue`, `--color-gray-700`, `--border`, `--font-mono`, `--transition-fast`)

**Design decisions:**

- Used bracket notation for CSS module access (`styles["toggle"]`) to satisfy `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` -- this returns `string | undefined` which is compatible with `className`
- The `disabled` prop uses `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`-safe typing (optional without `| undefined` in the type literal, which is the default for `?`)
- Thumb translation is `20px` (track width 40px minus thumb width 16px minus 2x border width 2px = 20px) to slide the indicator from left to right
- All styling uses CSS custom properties from the existing design token system

## Modal Spec
- Uses native `<dialog>` element
- Props: `open: boolean`, `onClose: () => void`, `title: string`, `children: React.ReactNode`
- Shows via `dialog.showModal()` when open is true (useEffect + useRef)
- Closes on Escape (native dialog behavior) - listen for 'close' event to call onClose
- Neo-Brutalist: thick 2px black border ON the dialog itself, no border-radius, dark background (gray-900), visible title bar with border-bottom, monospace font
- Backdrop: `dialog::backdrop { background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.85); }`
- Focus trap is native with `<dialog>`

2. Shows title text
3. Shows children content
4. Calls onClose when close event fires
5. Dialog is not visible when open=false

```ts
// In the test file, before the describe block:
beforeAll(() => {
  HTMLDialogElement.prototype.showModal = vi.fn();
  HTMLDialogElement.prototype.close = vi.fn();
});
```

All 12 tests pass. Here is a summary of what was implemented:

---

**Files created/modified:**

1. `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/ContextMenu.test.tsx` -- 12 tests covering all spec requirements
2. `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/ContextMenu.tsx` -- Component implementation
3. `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/ContextMenu.module.css` -- Neo-Brutalist styles

**Test coverage (12/12 passing):**
- Does not show menu by default
- Shows menu on `contextmenu` event (right-click)
- Menu has `role="menu"`
- Items have `role="menuitem"`
- Calls item action on click
- Closes menu after clicking an item
- Closes on Escape key
- Disabled items have `aria-disabled`
- Disabled items do not call action
- Closes when clicking outside
- Displays shortcut text when provided
- Renders children as the trigger area

**Key design decisions:**
- Named exports `ContextMenu` (component) and `MenuItem` (type) as specified
- All functions have explicit return types per ESLint rule
- `position: fixed` with mouse coordinates for menu placement

- `position: fixed` with mouse coordinates for menu placement

All clean. Here is the summary:

---

**Implementation complete.** Three files created:

- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Tooltip.test.tsx` -- 9 tests covering all spec requirements
- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Tooltip.tsx` -- Component implementation
- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/primitives/Tooltip.module.css` -- Neo-Brutalist styles

**Test coverage (9/9 passing):**
1. Does not show tooltip by default
2. Shows on mouseenter
3. Hides on mouseleave
4. Tooltip has `role="tooltip"`
5. Trigger child has `aria-describedby` pointing to tooltip id
6. Shows on focus
7. Hides on blur
8. Defaults placement to "top"
9. Applies specified placement class

**Key design decisions:**
- `cloneElement` injects `aria-describedby` directly onto the child element (not the wrapper span), satisfying the accessibility requirement that the trigger itself references the tooltip
- Wrapper span handles mouse/focus events via event bubbling
- `React.useId()` generates stable unique tooltip IDs
- CSS module classes are safely joined with `.filter(Boolean)` to handle `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` (indexed access returns `string | undefined`)
- Tooltip is conditionally rendered (not just hidden with CSS) so it does not appear in the accessibility tree when invisible
- `transition: opacity var(--transition-fast)` respects `prefers-reduced-motion` since `global.css` already sets `--transition-fast: 0ms` under that media query

**Verification:** `tsc --noEmit` clean, `eslint` clean, all tests green.

input.prompt:
Implement a RotaryKnob canvas-based control for a Neo-Brutalist DAW in /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/.

Tech: React 19, TypeScript strict (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes), Vitest, @testing-library/react.

Follow TDD: write tests FIRST, then implement.

## RotaryKnob Spec
Canvas-based rotary control with drag interaction.

Props:
```ts
type RotaryKnobProps = {
  value: number;
  min: number;
  max: number;
  step?: number;  // default 1
  onChange: (value: number) => void;
  label: string;
  size?: number;  // px, default 48
};
```

- Renders a `<canvas>` element inside a container div
- Canvas draws: outer circle (gray-700 border), arc from min to max showing value (blue accent), pointer line from center
- Below canvas: value text display (JetBrains Mono, text-xs)
- Above canvas: label text (text-xs, gray-300)
- Interaction: mousedown on canvas starts drag. Vertical mouse movement changes value (up = increase, down = decrease). Movement of 1px = 1 step. mouseup ends drag.
- Keyboard: Tab to focus the container, ArrowUp/ArrowRight = +step, ArrowDown/ArrowLeft = -step, PageUp = +10*step, PageDown = -10*step, Home = min, End = max
- ARIA: container div has `role="slider"`, `aria-valuemin`, `aria-valuemax`, `aria-valuenow`, `aria-label`
- tabIndex={0} on the container

For canvas colors, import from tokens.ts:
```ts
import { tokens } from "../tokens/tokens";
```
Use tokens.color.blue, tokens.color.gray700, tokens.color.white, tokens.color.gray300, tokens.color.black, tokens.border.width

## Test Requirements
1. Renders with correct ARIA attributes (role, valuemin, valuemax, valuenow, label)
2. Keyboard ArrowUp increases value by step
3. Keyboard ArrowDown decreases value by step
4. Keyboard Home sets to min, End sets to max
5. Value is clamped between min and max
6. Displays label text
7. Displays formatted value text

IMPORTANT for canvas testing: jsdom doesn't implement canvas. Mock getContext:
```ts
beforeAll(() => {
  HTMLCanvasElement.prototype.getContext = vi.fn(() => ({
    clearRect: vi.fn(),
    beginPath: vi.fn(),
    arc: vi.fn(),
    stroke: vi.fn(),
    moveTo: vi.fn(),
    lineTo: vi.fn(),
    fill: vi.fn(),
    save: vi.fn(),
    restore: vi.fn(),
    fillText: vi.fn(),
    measureText: vi.fn(() => ({ width: 0 })),
    translate: vi.fn(),
    rotate: vi.fn(),
    set strokeStyle(_v: string) {},
    set fillStyle(_v: string) {},
    set lineWidth(_v: number) {},
    set lineCap(_v: string) {},
    set font(_v: string) {},
    set textAlign(_v: string) {},
    set textBaseline(_v: string) {},
  })) as unknown as typeof HTMLCanvasElement.prototype.getContext;
});
```

## Important Rules
- File: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/RotaryKnob.tsx
- Test: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/RotaryKnob.test.tsx  
- Named export `RotaryKnob`
- eslint requires explicit return types
- For the draw function, use useEffect + useRef for the canvas ref
- No CSS module needed - use inline styles for layout (canvas doesn't need CSS pseudo-states)
- No emojis
- Run tests: cd /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness && pnpm vitest run src/ui/controls/RotaryKnob.test.tsx

Implement a Fader (vertical slider) component for a Neo-Brutalist DAW in /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/.

Tech: React 19, TypeScript strict (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes), Vitest, @testing-library/react.

Follow TDD: write tests FIRST, then implement.

## Fader Spec
Vertical slider control like a mixing console fader.

Props:
```ts
type FaderProps = {
  value: number;
  min: number;
  max: number;
  step?: number;  // default 1
  onChange: (value: number) => void;
  label: string;
  height?: number;  // px, default 128
};
```

- HTML-based (div track with draggable thumb), NOT canvas
- Vertical orientation: bottom = min, top = max
- Track: 4px wide centered strip, gray-700 background
- Active region: filled with blue from bottom up to thumb position
- Thumb: 12px wide, 8px tall square, white background with 2px gray-700 border
- Drag: mousedown on thumb starts drag, vertical mouse movement adjusts value, mouseup ends
- Keyboard: ArrowUp = +step, ArrowDown = -step, PageUp = +10*step, PageDown = -10*step, Home = min, End = max
- ARIA: container has role="slider", aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow, aria-orientation="vertical", aria-label
- tabIndex={0} on container

Use CSS module for styling: Fader.module.css

## Test Requirements
1. Renders with correct ARIA attributes
2. Has aria-orientation="vertical"
3. Keyboard ArrowUp increases value
4. Keyboard ArrowDown decreases value  
5. Home/End set min/max
6. Value is clamped
7. Displays label

input.prompt:
Implement a VuMeter canvas-based component for a Neo-Brutalist DAW in /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/.

Tech: React 19, TypeScript strict (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes), Vitest, @testing-library/react.

Follow TDD: write tests FIRST, then implement.

## VuMeter Spec
Canvas-based vertical bar meter for audio level display.

Props:
```ts
type VuMeterProps = {
  level: number;       // 0-1 normalized level
  peak?: number;       // 0-1 peak hold level
  clip?: boolean;      // clip indicator
  width?: number;      // px, default 12
  height?: number;     // px, default 128
};
```

- Read-only display (no interaction)
- Canvas renders: background (black), level bar (green below 0.7, amber 0.7-0.9, red above 0.9), peak hold line (thin white horizontal line at peak level that decays), clip indicator (small red square at top when clip=true)
- ARIA: role="img", aria-label describing the level (e.g. "Audio level: 75%")
- Uses requestAnimationFrame internally for smooth peak hold decay. But for the initial render, just draw based on props.
- Import tokens from "../tokens/tokens" for canvas colors

## Test Requirements
1. Renders canvas element
2. Has role="img"
3. Has aria-label with level info
4. Canvas has correct dimensions
5. Clip indicator reflected in aria-label

IMPORTANT: Mock canvas getContext same as RotaryKnob:
```ts
beforeAll(() => {
  HTMLCanvasElement.prototype.getContext = vi.fn(() => ({
    clearRect: vi.fn(),
    fillRect: vi.fn(),
    beginPath: vi.fn(),
    moveTo: vi.fn(),
    lineTo: vi.fn(),
    stroke: vi.fn(),
    save: vi.fn(),
    restore: vi.fn(),
    set strokeStyle(_v: string) {},
    set fillStyle(_v: string) {},
    set lineWidth(_v: number) {},
  })) as unknown as typeof HTMLCanvasElement.prototype.getContext;
});
```

## Important Rules
- File: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/VuMeter.tsx
- Test: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/VuMeter.test.tsx
- Named export `VuMeter`
- eslint requires explicit return types
- No CSS module needed (canvas + minimal inline styles)
- Use tokens.color.green, tokens.color.amber, tokens.color.red, tokens.color.white, tokens.color.black
- No emojis
- Run tests: cd /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness && pnpm vitest run src/ui/controls/VuMeter.test.tsx

Implement DrumPad and StepButton components for a Neo-Brutalist DAW in /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/.

Tech: React 19, TypeScript strict (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes), Vitest, @testing-library/react.

Follow TDD: write tests FIRST, then implement.

## DrumPad Spec
A trigger button styled as a square pad.

Props:
```ts
type DrumPadProps = {
  label: string;
  active?: boolean;      // toggle state (e.g., selected instrument)
  onTrigger: () => void;
  size?: number;         // px, default 48
};
```

- Renders a native `<button>` element
- Visual: filled square, accent color (pink) when active, gray-900 when inactive
- 2px solid border (gray-700), no border-radius
- Label centered in JetBrains Mono, text-xs, uppercase
- On trigger (click/Enter/Space): calls onTrigger
- ARIA: aria-label={label}, aria-pressed when active
- Use CSS module: DrumPad.module.css

### DrumPad Tests
1. Renders button with aria-label
2. Calls onTrigger on click
3. Has aria-pressed="true" when active
4. Has aria-pressed="false" when not active
5. Shows label text

## StepButton Spec
Toggle button for sequencer steps (16 per row).

Props:
```ts
type StepButtonProps = {
  active: boolean;
  current?: boolean;     // true when sequencer is on this step
  onToggle: () => void;
  index: number;         // step number (0-15) for aria-label
};
```

- Renders a native `<button>` element
- Visual: small square (24px), filled with accent color (amber) when active, gray-900 when inactive
- 2px solid border. When current=true, border becomes white (highlighting current step)
- No border-radius
- ARIA: aria-pressed={active}, aria-label="Step {index + 1}"
- Use CSS module: StepButton.module.css

### StepButton Tests
1. Renders button
2. Has aria-pressed matching active prop
3. Calls onToggle on click
4. Has aria-label with step number
5. Has current step visual indicator (test className contains "current" class when current=true)

Toggle button for sequencer steps (16 per row).

Props:
```ts
type StepButtonProps = {
  active: boolean;
  current?: boolean;     // true when sequencer is on this step
  onToggle: () => void;
  index: number;         // step number (0-15) for aria-label
};
```

- Renders a native `<button>` element
- Visual: small square (24px), filled with accent color (amber) when active, gray-900 when inactive
- 2px solid border. When current=true, border becomes white (highlighting current step)
- No border-radius
- ARIA: aria-pressed={active}, aria-label="Step {index + 1}"
- Use CSS module: StepButton.module.css

### StepButton Tests
1. Renders button
2. Has aria-pressed matching active prop
3. Calls onToggle on click
4. Has aria-label with step number
5. Has current step visual indicator (test className contains "current" class when current=true)

## DrumPad Spec
A trigger button styled as a square pad.

Props:
```ts
type DrumPadProps = {
  label: string;
  active?: boolean;      // toggle state (e.g., selected instrument)
  onTrigger: () => void;
  size?: number;         // px, default 48
};
```

- Renders a native `<button>` element
- Visual: filled square, accent color (pink) when active, gray-900 when inactive
- 2px solid border (gray-700), no border-radius
- Label centered in JetBrains Mono, text-xs, uppercase
- On trigger (click/Enter/Space): calls onTrigger
- ARIA: aria-label={label}, aria-pressed when active
- Use CSS module: DrumPad.module.css

### DrumPad Tests
1. Renders button with aria-label
2. Calls onTrigger on click
3. Has aria-pressed="true" when active
4. Has aria-pressed="false" when not active
5. Shows label text

## StepButton Spec

type StepButtonProps = {

Implement a RotaryKnob canvas-based control for a Neo-Brutalist DAW in /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/.

Tech: React 19, TypeScript strict (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes), Vitest, @testing-library/react.

Follow TDD: write tests FIRST, then implement.

## RotaryKnob Spec
Canvas-based rotary control with drag interaction.

Props:
```ts
type RotaryKnobProps = {
  value: number;
  min: number;
  max: number;
  step?: number;  // default 1
  onChange: (value: number) => void;
  label: string;
  size?: number;  // px, default 48
};
```

- Renders a `<canvas>` element inside a container div
- Canvas draws: outer circle (gray-700 border), arc from min to max showing value (blue accent), pointer line from center
- Below canvas: value text display (JetBrains Mono, text-xs)
- Above canvas: label text (text-xs, gray-300)
- Interaction: mousedown on canvas starts drag. Vertical mouse movement changes value (up = increase, down = decrease). Movement of 1px = 1 step. mouseup ends drag.
- Keyboard: Tab to focus the container, ArrowUp/ArrowRight = +step, ArrowDown/ArrowLeft = -step, PageUp = +10*step, PageDown = -10*step, Home = min, End = max
- ARIA: container div has `role="slider"`, `aria-valuemin`, `aria-valuemax`, `aria-valuenow`, `aria-label`
- tabIndex={0} on the container

For canvas colors, import from tokens.ts:
```ts
import { tokens } from "../tokens/tokens";
```
Use tokens.color.blue, tokens.color.gray700, tokens.color.white, tokens.color.gray300, tokens.color.black, tokens.border.width

## Test Requirements
1. Renders with correct ARIA attributes (role, valuemin, valuemax, valuenow, label)
2. Keyboard ArrowUp increases value by step
3. Keyboard ArrowDown decreases value by step
4. Keyboard Home sets to min, End sets to max
5. Value is clamped between min and max
6. Displays label text
7. Displays formatted value text

IMPORTANT for canvas testing: jsdom doesn't implement canvas. Mock getContext:
```ts
beforeAll(() => {
  HTMLCanvasElement.prototype.getContext = vi.fn(() => ({
    clearRect: vi.fn(),
    beginPath: vi.fn(),
    arc: vi.fn(),
    stroke: vi.fn(),
    moveTo: vi.fn(),
    lineTo: vi.fn(),
    fill: vi.fn(),
    save: vi.fn(),
    restore: vi.fn(),
    fillText: vi.fn(),
    measureText: vi.fn(() => ({ width: 0 })),
    translate: vi.fn(),
    rotate: vi.fn(),
    set strokeStyle(_v: string) {},
    set fillStyle(_v: string) {},
    set lineWidth(_v: number) {},
    set lineCap(_v: string) {},
    set font(_v: string) {},
    set textAlign(_v: string) {},
    set textBaseline(_v: string) {},
  })) as unknown as typeof HTMLCanvasElement.prototype.getContext;
});
```

## Important Rules
- File: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/RotaryKnob.tsx
- Test: /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/RotaryKnob.test.tsx  
- Named export `RotaryKnob`
- eslint requires explicit return types
- For the draw function, use useEffect + useRef for the canvas ref
- No CSS module needed - use inline styles for layout (canvas doesn't need CSS pseudo-states)
- No emojis
- Run tests: cd /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness && pnpm vitest run src/ui/controls/RotaryKnob.test.tsx

- Keyboard: ArrowUp = +step, ArrowDown = -step, PageUp = +10*step, PageDown = -10*step, Home = min, End = max
- ARIA: container has role="slider", aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow, aria-orientation="vertical", aria-label
- tabIndex={0} on container

- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/DrumPad.test.tsx` -- 5 tests covering aria-label, onTrigger click, aria-pressed states, and label text rendering

- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/DrumPad.module.css` -- Neo-Brutalist pad styles using design tokens (`--border`, `--color-pink`, `--color-gray-900`, `--font-mono`, `--text-xs`)
- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/StepButton.test.tsx` -- 5 tests covering button rendering, aria-pressed matching active prop, onToggle click, aria-label with step number, and current step class
- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/StepButton.tsx` -- Named export `StepButton` component: native `<button>`, amber accent when active, white border when current, aria-label "Step {n}"
- `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/StepButton.module.css` -- Sequencer step styles using design tokens (`--border`, `--color-amber`, `--color-gray-900`, `--color-white`)

The `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` constraint is respected by using `= false`/`= 48` defaults and never assigning `undefined` to optional props explicitly.

Implement DrumPad and StepButton components for a Neo-Brutalist DAW in /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/.

Tech: React 19, TypeScript strict (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes), Vitest, @testing-library/react.

Follow TDD: write tests FIRST, then implement.

## DrumPad Spec
A trigger button styled as a square pad.

Props:
```ts
type DrumPadProps = {
  label: string;
  active?: boolean;      // toggle state (e.g., selected instrument)
  onTrigger: () => void;
  size?: number;         // px, default 48
};
```

- Renders a native `<button>` element
- Visual: filled square, accent color (pink) when active, gray-900 when inactive
- 2px solid border (gray-700), no border-radius
- Label centered in JetBrains Mono, text-xs, uppercase
- On trigger (click/Enter/Space): calls onTrigger
- ARIA: aria-label={label}, aria-pressed when active
- Use CSS module: DrumPad.module.css

### DrumPad Tests
1. Renders button with aria-label
2. Calls onTrigger on click
3. Has aria-pressed="true" when active
4. Has aria-pressed="false" when not active
5. Shows label text

## StepButton Spec
Toggle button for sequencer steps (16 per row).

Props:
```ts
type StepButtonProps = {
  active: boolean;
  current?: boolean;     // true when sequencer is on this step
  onToggle: () => void;
  index: number;         // step number (0-15) for aria-label
};
```

- Renders a native `<button>` element
- Visual: small square (24px), filled with accent color (amber) when active, gray-900 when inactive
- 2px solid border. When current=true, border becomes white (highlighting current step)
- No border-radius
- ARIA: aria-pressed={active}, aria-label="Step {index + 1}"
- Use CSS module: StepButton.module.css

### StepButton Tests
1. Renders button
2. Has aria-pressed matching active prop
3. Calls onToggle on click
4. Has aria-label with step number
5. Has current step visual indicator (test className contains "current" class when current=true)

## Important Rules
- Files:
  - /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/DrumPad.tsx
  - /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/DrumPad.module.css

  - /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/ui/controls/StepButton.test.tsx
- Named exports: `DrumPad`, `StepButton`
- eslint requires explicit return types
- Use var(--color-*), var(--font-mono), var(--border), var(--text-xs)
- No emojis
- Run tests: cd /Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness && pnpm vitest run src/ui/controls/
